title: Shoe falling apart after 2 months, warranty denied
body: The shoe started falling apart in a couple months and the company wouldn't stand by their product. The sole on the right shoe had a large split going from side to side and part of the bottom of the shoe started delaminating. They said this was normal wear and tear and wouldn't warranty them. I've had cheap shoes from Walmart that have lasted longer. For a $160 pair of shoes this is ridiculous.

title: Most uncomfortable shoes I've owned, bought for plantar fasciitis
body: Bought a pair for my plantar fasciitis, and they were the most uncomfortable shoes I've ever owned. Did all the things they said to do, gave them a fair shot and they never were comfortable. Wasted my money because it was past the return time. Guess they count on that. Do not recommend.
personas: The Plantar Fasciitis & Heel Pain Sufferer

title: Sizing inconsistent between models
body: I bought a pair of Quantums and they felt hard as rocks. I had also purchased a pair of the Atoms and they were a little short so I returned them. I reordered the Atoms in a half size larger and they were huge. The sizing is not consistent between shoes. Very disappointed. I returned the second pair of Atoms. The Quantums will be my yard work shoes as I can't wear them for a walk.

title: Return refused, claim shoes were worn outside
body: I am writing to file a formal complaint against Kuru Footwear regarding their refusal to process a refund for a returned pair of shoes. The company claims the shoes were worn outside, a claim I vehemently dispute. I tried on the shoes exclusively indoors on a carpeted surface for a brief period to assess their fit and comfort. At no point did the shoes leave my home or come into contact with any outdoor surfaces.

title: Order never received, customer service never followed up
body: Worst customer service I have ever dealt with. I had ordered a pair of Atoms for my wife. The order was never received. After 3 phone calls to Kuru's customer service number, and being assured 'that a manager would contact me that day', I never heard a thing. No return call, no email, nothing! It appears as if they just don't care. I finally disputed the charge on my credit card.

title: Heel hugging gimmick, may be making plantar fasciitis worse
body: I bought a pair. I normally wear a 10.5. I bought an 11. It's a bit too short and my big toe keeps hitting the end of the shoe. I tried them out for a few weeks, but I don't think they're doing anything to help my PF. The 'hugging' heel is a gimmick IMO. I think they might actually be making it worse because the heel is raised a bit higher than my other shoes.
personas: The Plantar Fasciitis & Heel Pain Sufferer

title: Soles separating after 5-6 months, switched brands
body: I was an early Kuru fan but my last two pairs of Atoms have come apart where the black sole meets the white part. Each pair lasted me maybe 5-6 months with casual wear before they started separating. For $160 shoes that's just unacceptable. I've switched to another brand.

title: Return denied, claimed shoes were dirty, they never left the house
body: Difficult return and poor customer service. I received my shoes and after trying them on in my home they didn't feel any more comfortable than my current shoes. I returned them the next day via the QR code provided. Two weeks later I had to contact them to find out the status of my refund. They claimed they had not received them. The next day I get an email stating my return was denied because they were worn and dirty. These shoes never left my home. I feel this company is a scam.
